meaning: An official who makes sure that players follow the rules of a game.
example: The referee blew his whistle to stop the soccer match after a foul.

meaning: A punishment given to a player or team for breaking a rule.
example: The team received a penalty because one of their players touched the ball with their hands.

meaning: An unfair play or action in a game that goes against the rules.
example: Pushing another player is a foul and will result in a free kick.

meaning: A position in sports like soccer or hockey where a player is in an illegal spot, usually too close to the opponent's goal.
example: The striker scored a goal, but it did not count because he was offside.

meaning: Extra time played at the end of a game when the score is tied.
example: The basketball game went into overtime after both teams scored 90 points.

meaning: A series of games or contests in which many players or teams compete to win a final championship.
example: The tennis tournament lasts for two weeks and has over a hundred players.

meaning: A player who replaces another player during a game.
example: The coach decided to send in a substitute player in the second half of the match.

meaning: To stop someone from playing or competing in a game because they did not follow the rules.
example: The runner was disqualified for starting the race before the whistle blew.

meaning: Outside the playing area of a field or court.
example: The tennis ball landed out of bounds, so the point went to the other player.

meaning: The part of a team that tries to stop the other team from scoring points.
example: Our team has a strong defense, so it is difficult for opponents to score.

meaning: The part of a team that tries to score goals or points.
example: The team's offense is very fast and has scored many goals this season.

meaning: A game that ends with the same score for both teams; a tie.
example: The match ended in a 1-1 draw, so both teams received one point.

meaning: The final game or match to decide who is the best player or team in a sport.
example: They trained hard all year to win the state soccer championship.

meaning: A short rest period between the two halves of a sports game.
example: During halftime, the players drank water and listened to the coach's new plan.

meaning: A series of extra games played at the end of a regular season to decide the champion.
example: Only the top eight teams in the league qualify for the playoffs.

meaning: A person who watches a sports event or game.
example: Thousands of spectators filled the stadium to watch the final race.

meaning: A person who trains and guides athletes or teams.
example: The coach gave the team instructions on how to improve their passing.

meaning: The chosen leader of a sports team.
example: The captain shook hands with the referee before the game started.

meaning: Fair, polite, and honest behavior when playing a sport or game.
example: Shaking hands with your opponent after losing is a sign of good sportsmanship.

meaning: To lose a game automatically because you cannot play or because you broke a rule.
example: The team had to forfeit the game because they did not have enough players.

meaning: A player or team that is expected to lose a game or competition.
example: Everyone was surprised when the underdog team won the final match.

meaning: An abbreviation for Most Valuable Player, an award given to the best player in a game or season.
example: Sarah was named MVP after scoring three goals in the final match.

meaning: The ranking of players or teams in a tournament based on their past performance.
example: Because she has the highest seeding, she will play against lower-ranked players first.

meaning: A flat frame used for carrying an injured player off the field.
example: The player injured his leg and had to be carried away on a stretcher.

meaning: The sports field or stadium that belongs to a specific team.
example: Our team usually plays better when we are on our home turf.
